characters.physical_details: Dark curls, left-temple mole, callused hands, cheek-biting tell.
characters.occupation_status: CommonFrame claims adjuster through chapter 12; Blue Hour repair worker after; terminal triage, registry, and network coordinator by chapter 18.
characters.literacy_languages: Reads/writes English; fluent in claims code and repair paperwork; practical Spanish.

state_transitions.change: Copies LM-127B's residue packet to a hidden calibration wafer and keeps the body off pulp.
Begins steering renters into illegal repair work with Tavi.
Steals an archive mirror wafer with Warehouse Nine routing data.
Exposes bargain-line overlap production, loses her job and license, and takes a $5,000 fine.
Helps turn the old ferry terminal into an emergency repair site.
Runs triage and the registry for the terminal network.

characters.physical_details: Lean, bench-stooped, flattened nose, long hands, burn scar on right forearm.
characters.occupation_status: Former CommonFrame vat tech later moved to bargain-line yield work; Blue Hour repairer; terminal repair lead after insolvency.
characters.literacy_languages: Literate in English; fluent in vat maintenance, tissue repair, maker bulletins, and salvage improvisation.

state_transitions.change: Becomes Mira's lover and admits he helped set bargain-line residue tolerances.
Burns Blue Hour files and loses the wetbench in the raid fire.
Rebuilds repair capacity in the ferry terminal.

characters.physical_details: Adult bargain frame with one lighter iris, a pale healed scar under the left ribs, a left-ear notch, and a nape port.
characters.occupation_status: Starts as held model LM-127B; becomes a conscious overlap person, intake worker, registry witness, terminal organizer, and South Basin trainer.
characters.literacy_languages: Speaks English; reading and writing grow into registry and intake work.

state_transitions.change: Wakes on Tavi's bench and chooses the name Lin.
Receives a key and begins living at Brine Court.
Helps build the handwritten registry and donor-chain links.
Files witness testimony under the name Lin.
Enters the civic record as a protected disputed claimant.
Takes intake at the ferry terminal and starts public triage work.
Travels to South Basin and other rooms to teach intake and registry methods.

characters.availability: Hidden from authorities through chapter 14; public after filing witness testimony in chapter 15.

characters.physical_details: Silvering jaw-length hair, expensive pens, careful wool clothes, arthritic hands.

key_relationships.yaret_sanz: bureau ally until the case goes public

characters.occupation_status: CommonFrame claims director and internal source until insolvency; later supplies seed maps, maintenance schematics, and maker indexes.
characters.literacy_languages: Fluent in English; expert in contracts, archives, executive language, and maintenance paperwork.

state_transitions.change: Offers Mira reassignment, housing, and salary for silence.
Authenticates the seed-map archive at the tribunal.
Loses corporate housing and brings maintenance maps to the terminal.
Works the terminal maker index and maintenance substitutions.

characters.physical_details: Former dockworker build, lumbar brace, left-foot drag that worsens with fatigue or cold.
characters.occupation_status: Gray-market dock and freight worker using rented heavy-lift bodies; later terminal dispatch lead.
characters.literacy_languages: Literate in English; reads tide sheets, union boards, gig contracts, debt notices.

state_transitions.change: Explains how dependent his livelihood is on rented labor bodies.
Adds his declined North Quay scan-retention letter to the registry.
Takes a baton strike across old spinal damage in the Blue Hour raid.
Accepts a stronger pain patch under a fall-risk waiver after hospital observation.
Builds the ferry terminal dispatch desk from a chair and a notebook.

characters.availability: Present from chapter 5 onward; mobility worsens after chapter 14, then partly stabilizes once he shifts into dispatch work.

characters.physical_details: Silver braid, nicotine laugh, detergent-dried hands, practical shoes.

key_relationships.mira_alves: neighbor ally and later cooperative partner

characters.occupation_status: Owner of Blue Hour Laundry and neighborhood fixer; after the fire becomes quartermaster and crowd-control lead at the terminal.
characters.literacy_languages: Literate in English, conversational Tagalog and some Spanish, keeps accounts by hand.

state_transitions.change: Gives Mira and Tavi the Blue Hour back room and sets rules.
Loses Blue Hour to the raid and fire.
Breaks open the old ferry terminal and organizes triage.

characters.physical_details: Lean, close-trimmed, carefully still; courtesy used as pressure.

key_relationships.celia_rusk: bureau ally until the case goes public
key_relationships.lin: body he tries to keep as property

characters.occupation_status: Municipal Tissue Safety Bureau investigator over unlicensed repairs, seizures, and public-safety claims.
characters.literacy_languages: Fully literate in English; fluent in regulatory code, warrants, evidence procedure.

state_transitions.change: Opens the tissue-safety inquiry into Mira and Blue Hour.
Leads the Blue Hour raid.
Argues to exclude Lin and preserve overlap bodies as review material.
Treats terminal care as unauthorized salvage while losing Lin's custody.

characters.physical_details: Dock-broad shoulders narrowed by injury, steel walker with a pink-thread pouch, child's green barrette.
characters.occupation_status: Former dock worker and Pool Six rehab client; living donor witness at Pelican Courts.
characters.literacy_languages: Literate in English; reads rehab paperwork, debt notices, and payment plans.

state_transitions.change: Confirms that the orange Pool Six addendum let North Quay retain and reuse her gait archive.

characters.availability: Appears in chapter 13 and remains alive at Pelican Courts.

characters.physical_details: White raincoat, speckled courtroom grime by chapter 17, thumbnail chewed flat.
characters.occupation_status: Plaintiff-coalition lawyer handling tribunal and bankruptcy strategy around overlap claimants.
characters.literacy_languages: Literate in English; fluent in court procedure and emergency filings.

state_transitions.change: Moves from coalition messaging into direct terminal documentation and bankruptcy-hearing triage.

characters.physical_details: South Basin hotel worker with one silver-polished thumbnail; carries a red lunch tin and bus receipts.
characters.occupation_status: South Basin conference-hotel worker and Pelorus-shell witness who widens the registry beyond CommonFrame.
characters.literacy_languages: Literate in English; keeps contracts, schedules, and transit receipts.

state_transitions.change: Brings South Basin records and six Pelorus-linked names to the terminal; survives filament extraction and remains a contact.

characters.availability: Appears in chapter 18 and remains linked to the South Basin intake network.

timeline.story_start: Late August 2049, opening on a Friday return cycle at CommonFrame's Claims Annex in Cordera.
timeline.estimated_span: About five months from late summer to midwinter; chapter 18 adds a five-day jump.

key_events.event: Mira places LM-127B on hold and copies the first residue packet to a calibration wafer.
LM-127B wakes and takes the name Lin.
Mira steals an archive mirror wafer during the audit inquiry.
Warehouse Nine printouts prove deliberate donor seeding, retention bands, and recirculation.
Mira exposes CommonFrame at Yolanda Paredes's hearing, stays the claim, and loses her job and license.
Blue Hour starts the handwritten registry, and Marisol Velez confirms the Pool Six archive clause.
Lin files witness testimony under the name Lin.
The tribunal freezes new bargain production and creates protected disputed claimants.
Insolvency suspends maintenance; the old ferry terminal becomes an emergency repair and dispatch hub.
LM-304C survives an emergency terminal flush, but Tomas Vale's sister refuses a public witness role.
South Basin / St. Brigid becomes the first satellite intake room.
Pia Dizon's Pelorus shell proves the hidden-pocket practice extends beyond CommonFrame.
key_events.timing: Five days after South Basin outreach begins

geography.primary_setting: Cordera, a tidal Atlantic biotech city where leased bodies, salvage, bureaucracy, docks, and working-class housing share one tram-ferry grid.

key_locations.name: CommonFrame Claims Annex
key_locations.details: Cold office and intake lab on the industrial canal where returned bargain bodies are scored, billed, and routed to wash, salvage, or pulp.

key_locations.name: Blue Hour Laundry
key_locations.details: Neighborhood laundromat whose back room becomes the first illicit repair desk and later burns in the raid.

key_locations.name: Brine Court
key_locations.details: Mira and Gabe's damp third-floor walk-up uphill from the seawall; Lin's first refuge.

key_locations.name: Warehouse Nine / W9 Annex
key_locations.details: CommonFrame disposal site where shells are graded by yield, debt source, residual richness, and recirculation value.

key_locations.name: Municipal Arbitration Hall
key_locations.details: Civic-core hearing complex where Lin files testimony, Mira testifies, and the production-freeze order issues.

key_locations.name: Harbor Shift
key_locations.details: Labor-body booking office under the elevated tracks where workers reserve dock, cold-room, and stair-flex frames.

key_locations.name: Saint Meridian Hotel
key_locations.details: Hotel where Nidia Solis works and rents housekeeping frames for upper-floor labor.

key_locations.name: Pelican Courts
key_locations.details: North Quay spillover housing where Marisol Velez keeps the Pool Six rehab paperwork.

key_locations.name: Old Ferry Terminal
key_locations.details: Disused transit structure seized for emergency maintenance, beds, dispatch, and public intake after CommonFrame collapses; Gate Four becomes the live address.

key_locations.name: South Basin / St. Brigid basement
key_locations.details: Church-basement intake room and linked South Basin conference-hotel cases reached by working cutter; the first satellite site beyond the terminal.

key_locations.name: Harbor East
key_locations.details: Another district with overlap cases, reached through union contacts by chapter 18.

key_locations.name: East Seawall
key_locations.details: Open concrete steps, food carts, ferry horns, and wind; site of Gabe's labor-body explanation in chapter 10.

distances: Brine Court to Blue Hour is about a twelve-minute uphill walk.
Blue Hour to the Claims Annex is three tram stops down-canal.
Warehouse Nine lies farther along CommonFrame's industrial water, past the public salvage slips.
Harbor Shift is inland from the seawall under the elevated tracks, walkable from Brine Court.
Brine Court to the old ferry terminal is about twenty minutes downhill on foot.
The old ferry terminal is colder, larger, and closer to open water than Blue Hour.
South Basin rooms are reached from the terminal by working ferries and cutters.
Municipal Arbitration Hall is in the civic core, costly in time and fare for poor clients.

world_rules: People with money can project into licensed bioprinted bodies while their originals remain sedated elsewhere.
CommonFrame's bargain line intentionally uses aggregate lattice, salvage recomposition, debt-pool scan rights, and incompletely washed residue as inputs.
Debt and recovery contracts can hide scan-retention or therapeutic-modeling clauses, turning poor residents into donor sources without meaningful consent.
Unlicensed tissue repair is illegal and can trigger raids, seizures, license loss, and civil fines.
Before chapter 16, printed bodies are property in law; after it, living overlaps can testify and be treated as protected disputed claimants but still lack full personhood.
Insolvency and asset-preservation orders can suspend maintenance portals, parts, and new bargain production while active bargain users remain in circulation.
After official service collapses, survival depends on maker-specific schematics, salvaged parts, manual substitutions, and handwritten intake systems.

power_structure.over: bargain users, claims staff, donor-source stock
power_structure.mechanism: Contracts, service portals, debt riders, claims billing, and proprietary maintenance.

power_structure.over: claims workflow and internal records before insolvency
power_structure.mechanism: Archive access, litigation prep, selective disclosure, and management authority.

power_structure.over: repair rooms, biological stock, and alleged public-safety risks
power_structure.mechanism: Licensing, raids, seizures, civil fines, and evidence-control claims.

power_structure.over: CommonFrame production, overlap stock, and claimant status
power_structure.mechanism: Continuances, preservation orders, production freezes, disputed-claimant classifications, and maintenance-continuity orders.

power_structure.over: active bargain users and maintenance depots after chapter 16
power_structure.mechanism: Service suspensions, parts holdbacks, portal shutdowns, and asset-preservation review.

power_structure.over: access to scarce care after insolvency
power_structure.mechanism: Handwritten intake, dispatch routing, pooled supplies, maintenance knowledge, and bed allocation.

objects.name: LM-127B / Lin's body
objects.owner: CommonFrame at introduction; later Lin under protected disputed-claimant status
objects.origin: CommonFrame bargain-line body diverted off pulp after Mira's hold.
objects.status: Wakes in chapter 4, survives the raid, enters the civic record in chapters 15-16, and remains active through chapter 18.

objects.name: Calibration wafer
objects.owner: Mira Alves; later part of Blue Hour's evidence chain
objects.origin: Copied from LM-127B's deep residue review during hold processing.
objects.status: Hidden proof that bargain stock carries structured memory, not random wash noise.

objects.name: Archive mirror wafer
objects.owner: Mira Alves, then Blue Hour and the registry chain
objects.origin: Stolen from CommonFrame records during the audit inquiry.
objects.status: Carries Warehouse Nine routing and archive data used to access seed and yield records.

objects.name: Warehouse Nine printouts
objects.owner: Mira Alves, then Blue Hour evidence chain
objects.origin: Torn from the W9 office printer during the chapter 9 break-in.
objects.status: Partial paper proof of D-pool sourcing, target retention bands, and recirculation logic; enough to stop calling overlap contamination but not enough to show every sign-off.

objects.name: Handwritten registry ledger
objects.owner: Shared by Mira, Lin, Gabe, Tavi, Jo, then the terminal network
objects.origin: Built from laundry tickets, notebooks, donor interviews, and copied clauses once digital evidence became unsafe.
objects.status: Escapes the Blue Hour raid, enters the tribunal as pattern evidence, gains a Pelorus tab in chapter 18, and remains the cooperative's master record.

objects.name: Seed map archive
objects.owner: Held privately by Celia Rusk until the tribunal
objects.origin: Executive backup archive showing donor aggregation, retention scoring, and bargain-line seeding.
objects.status: Authenticated in chapter 16 and used to prove deliberate production management.

objects.name: Maintenance map folio
objects.owner: Celia Rusk, then the terminal cooperative
objects.origin: Archived service schematics, maker bulletins, and manual overrides pulled before Celia loses access.
objects.status: Used from chapter 17 onward to keep bargain users functioning after official maintenance shuts down.

objects.name: Tavi's wetbench
objects.origin: Salvaged repair equipment assembled from old clinic stock and vat parts.
objects.status: Central to Blue Hour until destroyed in the chapter 14 raid and fire.

objects.name: Pelorus filament sample
objects.origin: Removed from Pia Dizon's Pelorus leisure shell in chapter 18.
objects.status: Physical proof that the hidden access-pocket logic extends beyond CommonFrame.

debts.deadline: Monthly rent; eviction pressure escalates with arrears
debts.status: Active by chapter 18; terminal income covers only part of the newest threat letter.

debts.amount: $7,800 tied to Gabe's earlier heavy-lift frame damage
debts.deadline: Collectible whenever Gabe takes traceable wage work
debts.status: Unpaid throughout; one reason Gabe stays in gray-market or dispatch work.

debts.amount: $12,840 plus a proposed 36-month bargain-lease suspension against Yolanda Paredes
debts.status: Stayed pending special review after Mira's testimony.

income_sources.reliability: Reliable until Mira is fired in chapter 12.

income_sources.source: Blue Hour repair cash and barter
income_sources.amount: Small cash plus food, transit credit, towels, dye, and vouchers
income_sources.reliability: Growing through chapter 13, then destroyed by the raid and fire.

income_sources.source: Gabe's gray-market dock shifts in rented heavy-lift bodies
income_sources.amount: About $300-$900 per shift depending on berth and danger
income_sources.reliability: Unstable before the raid and sharply reduced afterward.

income_sources.source: Terminal cooperative pooled fees, donations, and barter
income_sources.amount: Small cash amounts plus food, blankets, transit credit, tools, and parts
income_sources.reliability: Precarious but active from chapter 17 onward.

knowledge_state.knows: Lin is conscious and not salvage.
He set bargain carry thresholds and residue tolerances for the cheap line and knew overlap was engineered.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Mira until chapter 7; the public until later hearings

knowledge_state.knows: Lin is living hidden at Brine Court.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Landlord, creditors, and authorities until Lin goes public

knowledge_state.knows: Warehouse Nine sorts and reseeds shells by debt source, residual richness, and target retention bands, not damage class.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Public and most authorities until chapter 16

knowledge_state.knows: Her Pool Six rehab addendum let North Quay retain and reuse her gait archive in later bodies.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Public systems until the registry and later hearings expose the clause

knowledge_state.knows: Marisol Velez / Pool Six is a reused living donor trace linked to multiple overlap entries.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Most donor families and city systems before registry expansion

knowledge_state.knows: The registry can link overlaps to living donor chains and debt pools.
Seed maps prove deliberate donor aggregation, residual retention, and bargain-line seeding.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Claims staff, customers, and regulators until the tribunal

knowledge_state.knows: CommonFrame can use insolvency and service shutdown to preserve assets while active bargain users lose maintenance.
Pelorus budget shells use the same hidden access-pocket logic seen in CommonFrame cases.
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Public courts and most clients at cycle end

environmental_constants: Cordera air usually carries salt, fryer grease, machine coolant, and detergent.
CommonFrame interiors smell filtered and cold until sweet tissue rot and solvent break through.
Blue Hour's back room is warm, damp, steam-softened, and dryer-loud while it stands.
Brine Court carries damp mineral air, radiator knocks, and tram noise through thin walls.
Warehouse Nine is humid-cold and smells of bleach, ammonia, old coffee, warm protein, and denature runoff.
The old ferry terminal is drafty, saline, colder than Blue Hour, and by midwinter smells of broth, bleach, wet wool, rust, and space heaters.
Official rooms like arbitration halls and hospitals smell of old coffee, wax, toner, and overmanaged temperature.
